title: Threat Models, Untrusted Content, XSS, Paths, Templates, and CSP · Language Clinic: Markdown Html Policy
summary: Raw HTML executes author input, javascript links survive, a template loader escapes its root, copied SVG contains active content, secrets enter output, and CSP is treated as the only XSS defense. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the markdown html policy increment in explicit static-publishing trust and security policy. Learners progress through…
objectives: Disable or sanitize raw HTML according to content trust and parser configuration and test dangerous tags, attributes, URLs, and DOM identifiers.
Identify content authors, reviewers, build runner, dependencies, templates, assets, output consumers, hosting controls, protected data, and realistic attackers.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-security-trust-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_security_trust_3(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Markdown Html Policy evidence · Theory Markdown Html Policy
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-markdown-html-policy-ani1cu" that uses case 1944 to enforce content, path, template, URL, secret, and defense-in-depth trust boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Markdown Html Policy evidence · Theory Markdown Html Policy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Markdown Html Policy into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Markdown Html Policy evidence · Theory Markdown Html Policy, keep Markdown Html Policy connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-markdown-html-policy-ani1cu
# Competency: ssg-markdown-html-policy.
# Case 1944: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_markdown_html_policy_ani1cu(untrusted_cases: int = 12, rejected_or_encoded: int = 12, path_escapes: int = 0, secret_findings: int = 0, unsafe_urls: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_ani1cu = "ani1cu-uc1ina-1944"
    assert evidence_variant_ani1cu
    evidence_axes_ani1cu = (
        "readerani1cu",
        "source1944",
        "parseuc1ina",
        "routeani1cu1944",
        "artifact1944ani1cu",
        "recoveryuc1ina1944",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_ani1cu)) == 6
    if untrusted_cases < 1 or rejected_or_encoded < 0:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "threat-cases"
    if rejected_or_encoded != untrusted_cases:
        return False, rejected_or_encoded, "injection-coverage"
    if path_escapes > 0 or secret_findings > 0:
        return False, path_escapes + secret_findings, "publication-leak"
    if unsafe_urls > 0:
        return False, unsafe_urls, "unsafe-url"
    assert rejected_or_encoded == untrusted_cases
    return True, rejected_or_encoded, "bounded-trust-policy"

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
